vocab.meaning: diễn ra trơn tru, đều đặn như bộ máy đồng hồ
vocab.example: Wearing a watch helps me ensure my daily schedule runs like clockwork, preventing any overlapping appointments.

vocab.meaning: cuộc đua với thời gian (làm việc gì đó gấp rút)
vocab.example: In my line of work, we are often in a race against time, so having the hour visible on my wrist is vital for meeting deadlines.
Some high-tech smartwatches are truly ahead of their time, offering features that we once only saw in science fiction movies.

vocab.meaning: có một khoảng thời gian cực kỳ vui vẻ
vocab.example: I don't think a watch is necessary when you're having the time of your life on vacation; in those moments, the clock doesn't matter.

vocab.word: lost in the mists of time
vocab.meaning: bị lãng quên do đã xảy ra từ quá lâu
vocab.example: The tradition of gifting pocket watches is somewhat lost in the mists of time, but the importance of punctuality remains modern.

vocab.meaning: cứ thong thả, không cần vội vã
vocab.example: Having a watch actually allows me to take my time and enjoy my surroundings because I know exactly how many minutes I have left.

vocab.meaning: giết thời gian (làm gì đó cho qua giờ lúc rảnh)
vocab.example: When I'm waiting for a bus, I check my watch to see if I have enough time to kill time by browsing a nearby bookstore.

vocab.meaning: kiên nhẫn chờ đợi thời cơ
vocab.example: I'm biding my time until I can afford a luxury watch, as I believe such a significant purchase shouldn't be rushed.

vocab.meaning: đang rất vội, không có nhiều thời gian
vocab.example: When I am pressed for time, a quick glance at my wrist is much more efficient than fumbling for my phone in my pocket.

vocab.meaning: vào phút chót, giây phút cuối cùng
vocab.example: Without a watch, I might end up finishing my tasks at the eleventh hour, which causes unnecessary stress and anxiety.
